howItWorks.intro: This coast fire calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Tax laws and financial markets change frequently. Verify current rates with your financial institution.

faq.question: What is Coast FIRE?
faq.answer: Coast FIRE means you have saved enough that compound growth alone will carry you to your retirement target — no more contributions needed. You still need to cover current expenses.

faq.question: How is Coast FIRE different from FIRE?
faq.answer: FIRE means you can stop working entirely. Coast FIRE means you can stop saving for retirement but still need income for living expenses. You can switch to a lower-paying, more fulfilling job.

faq.question: What assumptions matter most?
faq.answer: Return rate has the biggest impact. A 1% difference over 30 years changes the outcome dramatically. Use conservative estimates (6-7% nominal).

faq.question: Is this realistic?
faq.answer: Historically, a diversified stock portfolio has returned about 10% nominally. After inflation, about 7%. Coast FIRE works if you stay invested long-term and do not panic sell.

faq.question: Is the Coast FIRE Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

sections.title: What Is the Coast FIRE Calculator?
sections.body: The Coast FIRE Calculator is a free online tool designed for individuals and families who need quick, accurate calculations in the financial planning space. By entering your current savings, retirement goal, expected return, you get instant results including projected value at retirement, coast fire status, amount needed now to coast. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting projected value at retirement right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In financial planning, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by individuals and families worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to manage your finances with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your current savings and need to find the right projected value at retirement. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Current Savings ($)
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your current savings — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: retirement goal, expected return, retirement age, current age. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your projected value at retirement, coast fire status, amount needed now to coast. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your current savings before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both projected value at retirement and coast fire status — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to manage your finances.
If you're unsure about your current age, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.
